title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS · Language Lens: Tls Record Handshake Boundary
summary: A service enables TLS without certificate-name validation, chooses HTTP after reading encrypted application bytes, allows unsafe versions, and treats encryption as authorization and content integrity proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tls record handshake boundary increment in TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.
Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
